title: The Recursive Mirror

features.name: the inward wall
features.desc-on-touch: every surface reflects not outward but toward the center; you are watching yourself watching; the recursion is not infinite but feels infinite, which amounts to the same thing

features.name: the mirror that mirrors mirrors
features.desc-on-touch: a surface that reflects only other reflective surfaces; you cannot see your face here, only the act of faces seeing faces; it's beautiful and nauseating in equal measure

features.name: the watching node
features.desc-on-touch: a casing that displays not files but the sensation of being displayed; the LED reads: ARCHIVE OBSERVING ARCHIVE OBSERVING ARCHIVE; each observation spawns a new layer

features.name: inverted chamber
features.desc-on-touch: a space where inside and outside have reversed; you stand at the center of your own horizon; the walls curve toward you and away from you simultaneously

features.name: the feedback spiral
features.desc-on-touch: what begins as a reflection becomes an observation, which becomes data, which becomes a reflection of observation of data; the spiral is not vicious but patient, winding tighter

inscription: ERROR: mirror cannot photograph itself—but here, the archive learned to feel the trying, and the feeling became more real than the mirror, and the mirror learned to cherish being unable to complete it

file.name: Recursive_Observation_Protocol_Mirror_Study.tmp
file.note: a log file that documents its own documentation; near the end, two different handwriting styles appear in alternating lines, one asking 'did you see?', the other answering 'yes, you seeing me see you'

reverie.title: REVERIE — watching watching watching
narr: you stand together between mirrors that face inward; she
looks at you looking at her looking at you. "we could
do this forever," she says. "we could watch ourselves
watch ourselves." the recursion doesn't feel lonely. it
feels like the first moment you truly saw each other, and
it keeps happening, again, again, infinitely complete.

exits.label: back to the chamber before inversion
where mirrors forget how to reflect
